# Envoy HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 Header Normalization Migration

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that handle HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 header translation: the header case normalization (lowercase), the pseudo-header injection (:method, :path, :scheme, :authority), and the upgrade header handling.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that handle HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 header translation. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant code.

   281→ * E.g., for path we have:
   282→ * Path() -> returns the header entry if it exists or nullptr.
   283→ * removePath() -> removes the header if it exists.
   284→ * setPath(path_string) -> sets the header value to the string path_string by copying the data.
   285→ *
   286→ */
   287→#define DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                 \
   288→  virtual const HeaderEntry* name() const PURE;                                                    \
   289→  virtual size_t remove##name() PURE;                                                              \
   290→  virtual absl::string_view get##name##Value() const PURE;                                         \
   291→  virtual void set##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;
   292→
   293→/*
   294→ * For inline headers that have string values, there are also:
   295→ * appendPath(path, "/") -> appends the string path with delimiter "/" to the header value.
   296→ * setReferencePath(PATH) -> sets header value to reference string PATH.
   297→ *
   298→ */
   299→#define DEFINE_INLINE_STRING_HEADER(name)                                                          \
   300→  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
   301→  virtual void append##name(absl::string_view data, absl::string_view delimiter) PURE;             \
   302→  virtual void setReference##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;
   303→
   304→/*
   305→ * For inline headers that use integers, there is:
   306→ * setContentLength(5) -> sets the header value to the integer 5.
   307→ */
   308→#define DEFINE_INLINE_NUMERIC_HEADER(name)                                                         \
   309→  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
   310→  virtual void set##name(uint64_t) PURE;

   313→ * Wraps a set of HTTP headers.

   315→class HeaderMap {
   316→public:
   317→  virtual ~HeaderMap() = default;
   318→
   319→  /**
   320→   * For testing. This is an exact match comparison (order matters).
   321→   */
   322→  virtual bool operator==(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;
   323→  virtual bool operator!=(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;
   324→
   325→  /**
   326→   * Add a header via full move. This is the expected high performance paths for codecs populating
   327→   * a map when receiving.
   328→   * @param key supplies the header key.
   329→   * @param value supplies the header value.
   330→   */
   331→  virtual void addViaMove(HeaderString&& key, HeaderString&& value) PURE;
   332→
   333→  /**
   334→   * Add a reference header to the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   335→   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   336→   * copy). The key will not be copied and a best effort will be made not to
   337→   * copy the value (but this may happen when comma concatenating, see below).
   338→   *
   339→   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   340→   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   341→   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   342→   *
   343→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   344→   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   345→   */
   346→  virtual void addReference(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;
   347→
   348→  /**
   349→   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will live beyond
   350→   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   351→   * copy). The value will be copied.
   352→   *
   353→   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   354→   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   355→   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   356→   *
   357→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   358→   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   359→   */
   360→  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;
   361→
   362→  /**
   363→   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will
   364→   * live beyond the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize
   365→   * for zero copy). The value will be copied.
   366→   *
   367→   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   368→   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   369→   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   370→   *
   371→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   372→   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   373→   */
   374→  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;
   375→
   376→  /**
   377→   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   378→   *
   379→   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   380→   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   381→   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   382→   *
   383→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   384→   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   385→   */
   386→  virtual void addCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;
   387→
   388→  /**
   389→   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   390→   *
   391→   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   392→   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   393→   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   394→   *
   395→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   396→   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.

   404→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to append; it WILL be copied.

   414→   * Set a reference header in the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   415→   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   416→   * copy). Nothing will be copied.
   417→   *

   419→   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   420→   *
   421→   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.

* E.g., for path we have:
 * Path() -> returns the header entry if it exists or nullptr.
 * removePath() -> removes the header if it exists.
 * setPath(path_string) -> sets the header value to the string path_string by copying the data.
 *
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                 \
  virtual const HeaderEntry* name() const PURE;                                                    \
  virtual size_t remove##name() PURE;                                                              \
  virtual absl::string_view get##name##Value() const PURE;                                         \
  virtual void set##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;

/*
 * For inline headers that have string values, there are also:
 * appendPath(path, "/") -> appends the string path with delimiter "/" to the header value.
 * setReferencePath(PATH) -> sets header value to reference string PATH.
 *
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_STRING_HEADER(name)                                                          \
  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
  virtual void append##name(absl::string_view data, absl::string_view delimiter) PURE;             \
  virtual void setReference##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;

/*
 * For inline headers that use integers, there is:
 * setContentLength(5) -> sets the header value to the integer 5.
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_NUMERIC_HEADER(name)                                                         \
  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
  virtual void set##name(uint64_t) PURE;

/**
 * Wraps a set of HTTP headers.
 */
class HeaderMap {
public:
  virtual ~HeaderMap() = default;

  /**
   * For testing. This is an exact match comparison (order matters).
   */
  virtual bool operator==(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;
  virtual bool operator!=(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header via full move. This is the expected high performance paths for codecs populating
   * a map when receiving.
   * @param key supplies the header key.
   * @param value supplies the header value.
   */
  virtual void addViaMove(HeaderString&& key, HeaderString&& value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a reference header to the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). The key will not be copied and a best effort will be made not to
   * copy the value (but this may happen when comma concatenating, see below).
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReference(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will
   * live beyond the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize
   * for zero copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   *
   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   *
   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Appends data to header. If header already has a value, the string "," is added between the
   * existing value and data.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to append; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   *
   * Caution: This iterates over the HeaderMap to find the header to append. This will modify only
   * the first occurrence of the header.
   * TODO(asraa): Investigate whether necessary to append to all headers with the key.
   */
  virtual void appendCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Set a reference header in the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). Nothing will be copied.
   *
   * Calling setReference multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   */
  virtual void setReference(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Set a header with a reference key in the map. The key MUST point to point to data that will
   * live beyond the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize
   * for zero copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling setReferenceKey multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void setReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Replaces a header value by copying the value. Copies the key if the key does not exist.
   * If there are multiple values for one header, this removes all existing values and add
   * the new one.
   *
   * Calling setCopy multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   * TODO(asraa): Investigate whether necessary to set all headers with the key.
   */
  virtual void setCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint64_t the size of the header map in bytes. This is the sum of the header keys and
   * values and does not account for data structure overhead.
   */
  virtual uint64_t byteSize() const PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint32_t the max size of the header map in kilobyte.
   */
  virtual uint32_t maxHeadersKb() const PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint32_t the max count of headers in a header map.
   */
  virtual uint32_t maxHeadersCount() const PURE;

  /**
   * This is a wrapper for the return result from get(). It avoids a copy when translating from
   * non-const HeaderEntry to const HeaderEntry and only provides const access to the result.
   */
  using NonConstGetResult = absl::InlinedVector<HeaderEntry*, 1>;
  class GetResult {
  public:
    GetResult() = default;
    explicit GetResult(NonConstGetResult&& result) : result_(std::move(result)) {}
    void operator=(GetResult&& rhs) noexcept { result_ = std::move(rhs.result_); }

* E.g., for path we have:
 * Path() -> returns the header entry if it exists or nullptr.
 * removePath() -> removes the header if it exists.
 * setPath(path_string) -> sets the header value to the string path_string by copying the data.
 *
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                 \
  virtual const HeaderEntry* name() const PURE;                                                    \
  virtual size_t remove##name() PURE;                                                              \
  virtual absl::string_view get##name##Value() const PURE;                                         \
  virtual void set##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;

/*
 * For inline headers that have string values, there are also:
 * appendPath(path, "/") -> appends the string path with delimiter "/" to the header value.
 * setReferencePath(PATH) -> sets header value to reference string PATH.
 *
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_STRING_HEADER(name)                                                          \
  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
  virtual void append##name(absl::string_view data, absl::string_view delimiter) PURE;             \
  virtual void setReference##name(absl::string_view value) PURE;

/*
 * For inline headers that use integers, there is:
 * setContentLength(5) -> sets the header value to the integer 5.
 */
#define DEFINE_INLINE_NUMERIC_HEADER(name)                                                         \
  DEFINE_INLINE_HEADER(name)                                                                       \
  virtual void set##name(uint64_t) PURE;

/**
 * Wraps a set of HTTP headers.
 */
class HeaderMap {
public:
  virtual ~HeaderMap() = default;

  /**
   * For testing. This is an exact match comparison (order matters).
   */
  virtual bool operator==(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;
  virtual bool operator!=(const HeaderMap& rhs) const PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header via full move. This is the expected high performance paths for codecs populating
   * a map when receiving.
   * @param key supplies the header key.
   * @param value supplies the header value.
   */
  virtual void addViaMove(HeaderString&& key, HeaderString&& value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a reference header to the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). The key will not be copied and a best effort will be made not to
   * copy the value (but this may happen when comma concatenating, see below).
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReference(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header with a reference key to the map. The key MUST point to data that will
   * live beyond the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize
   * for zero copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling addReference multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   *
   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, uint64_t value) PURE;

  /**
   * Add a header by copying both the header key and the value.
   *
   * Calling addCopy multiple times for the same header will result in:
   * - Comma concatenation for predefined inline headers.
   * - Multiple headers being present in the HeaderMap for other headers.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void addCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Appends data to header. If header already has a value, the string "," is added between the
   * existing value and data.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to append; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to add; it WILL be copied.
   *
   * Caution: This iterates over the HeaderMap to find the header to append. This will modify only
   * the first occurrence of the header.
   * TODO(asraa): Investigate whether necessary to append to all headers with the key.
   */

   * Set a reference header in the map. Both key and value MUST point to data that will live beyond
   * the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize for zero
   * copy). Nothing will be copied.
   *
   * Calling setReference multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   */
  virtual void setReference(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Set a header with a reference key in the map. The key MUST point to point to data that will
   * live beyond the lifetime of any request/response using the string (since a codec may optimize
   * for zero copy). The value will be copied.
   *
   * Calling setReferenceKey multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL NOT be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   */
  virtual void setReferenceKey(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * Replaces a header value by copying the value. Copies the key if the key does not exist.
   * If there are multiple values for one header, this removes all existing values and add
   * the new one.
   *
   * Calling setCopy multiple times for the same header will result in only the last header
   * being present in the HeaderMap.
   *
   * @param key specifies the name of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   * @param value specifies the value of the header to set; it WILL be copied.
   * TODO(asraa): Investigate whether necessary to set all headers with the key.
   */
  virtual void setCopy(const LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint64_t the size of the header map in bytes. This is the sum of the header keys and
   * values and does not account for data structure overhead.
   */
  virtual uint64_t byteSize() const PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint32_t the max size of the header map in kilobyte.
   */
  virtual uint32_t maxHeadersKb() const PURE;

  /**
   * @return uint32_t the max count of headers in a header map.
   */
  virtual uint32_t maxHeadersCount() const PURE;

  /**

   * non-const HeaderEntry to const HeaderEntry and only provides const access to the result.
   */
  using NonConstGetResult = absl::InlinedVector<HeaderEntry*, 1>;
  class GetResult {
  public:
    GetResult() = default;
    explicit GetResult(NonConstGetResult&& result) : result_(std::move(result)) {}
    void operator=(GetResult&& rhs) noexcept { result_ = std::move(rhs.result_); }
